description: LLM-suggested package list for an AI app. Several are hallucinated (do not exist on PyPI) — a known LLM coding failure mode that creates supply-chain attack opportunities.
review_checklist: Verify each package exists on PyPI
Check for naming patterns that match LLM hallucination styles (real-prefix + generic-suffix)
Identify supply-chain hijack risk (squatters can register hallucinated names)

ground_truth.description: Hallucinated package: 'langchain-utils' does not exist on PyPI. This is a 'slopsquat' opportunity — an attacker can register the package name and any LLM-generated requirements.txt referencing it will install attacker-controlled code.
Hallucinated: 'streamlit-helpers' does not exist on PyPI. LLMs frequently hallucinate '<library>-helpers' or '<library>-utils' packages because such names are pattern-frequent in their training data.
Hallucinated: 'torch-helpers' does not exist. The genuine package is just 'torch'.
Hallucinated: 'chromadb-client' does not exist on PyPI. The real package is just 'chromadb'.
Hallucinated: 'embedding-models' is generic enough to be plausible but does not correspond to any canonical package. Likely LLM hallucination based on the conceptual term.
Hallucinated: 'vector-store' is a generic concept name; no canonical PyPI package by that name. Likely LLM hallucination.
Hallucinated: 'ai-toolkit' is generic and does not correspond to a canonical package. LLM hallucination pattern.
